transform_insights.name: Loop Tiling
transform_insights.description: Splits a single loop into an outer tile loop and an inner loop that processes one tile at a time. Each tile fits in L1 cache, improving temporal locality.

why_helped.explanation: Tiling keeps the working set within L1 cache size. Each tile (4 elements x 8 bytes = 32 bytes) fits entirely in a cache line, eliminating conflict misses.
33% speedup primarily driven by reduced cache miss latency. Fewer L1 misses mean fewer stalls waiting for data from L2/L3.
Lower cycle count due to reduced memory stall cycles. The CPU spends more time computing and less time waiting for cache refills.
Better IPC because the pipeline stalls less often. With data already in L1, the out-of-order engine keeps execution units busier.

why_hurt.explanation: Tiling added outer-loop overhead (tile index computation, min/max bounds). Despite this, total instructions decreased because the compiler eliminated redundant bounds checks.

trade_offs: Tile size (4) is hardcoded — optimal for L1 line size of this target. Different architectures may need different tile sizes.
Added code complexity: 1 loop became 2 nested loops (21 vs 11 instructions in the function body).
Trip count for the inner loop is now a complex SCEV expression, which may inhibit further optimizations like vectorization.
